rows.sat_rat: Mode presets and procedural checks reduce mis-selection, but manual mode choice still leaves room for human error.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of variation in particulate levels across a zone throughout a production run
rows.imp_rat: Uniform particulate levels across a zone matter for consistent classification, though spot breaches concern the operator more than gradients.
rows.sat_rat: Good air-change rates and diffuser layouts keep zones fairly uniform, but dead spots near equipment still show variation.

rows.stmt: Minimize the time to bring all zones to production-ready conditions ahead of a batch start
rows.imp_rat: Startup time affects batch schedule productivity but is a planning matter rather than a contamination risk to the operator's core job.
rows.sat_rat: Recovery-time behavior is well characterized and predictable, so operators typically bring zones ready within expected windows.

rows.source_job: feel in control of the environment
rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of uncertainty about whether air conditions are currently holding within spec across every zone
rows.imp_rat: Feeling confident that all zones are currently in spec gives the operator a sense of control central to their emotional job.
rows.sat_rat: Real-time dashboards give a broad picture of zone status, but blind spots and trust in sensors leave lingering uncertainty.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of pressure and airflow overshoot that occurs while transitioning to a new production mode
rows.imp_rat: Pressure/airflow overshoot during transitions is a nuisance that can briefly stress the cascade but usually self-corrects.
rows.sat_rat: PID tuning limits overshoot but is rarely optimized per mode, so some overshoot is routinely tolerated today.

rows.stmt: Reduce the time spent reconstructing or correcting the operating-condition record after the fact
rows.imp_rat: Time spent reconstructing the record after the fact is an administrative burden, not a contamination risk, so it ranks lower for the operator.
rows.sat_rat: Automated historians reduce reconstruction work, but chasing down and annotating anomalies still consumes operator time.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of uncontrolled pressure and airflow swings while bringing the system into idle
rows.imp_rat: Uncontrolled swings into idle risk stirring settled particulate but occur outside active production, lowering the stakes.
rows.sat_rat: Idle-down ramping is often only coarsely controlled, so some airflow surging during shutdown is common.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of failed start-up attempts that require restarting the air handling system before a batch
rows.imp_rat: Failed startup attempts delay batch start and annoy the operator but are recoverable before any product is at risk.
rows.sat_rat: Startup sequences are largely automated and reliable, so repeated failed attempts are the exception rather than the rule.

rows.source_job: feel in control of the environment
rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of moments when the operator feels the environment is behaving unpredictably during a run
rows.imp_rat: Moments when the environment feels unpredictable erode the operator's sense of mastery, a secondary emotional concern to functional control.
rows.sat_rat: Stable modern systems feel controllable most of the time, but transient upsets can still make behavior feel erratic.

rows.stmt: Reduce the frequency of re-verifying filtration after an inconclusive integrity check
rows.imp_rat: Re-verifying after an inconclusive integrity check is rework that delays zone release but does not itself threaten cleanliness.
rows.sat_rat: Inconclusive results still occur with current test methods, forcing repeat scans that the status quo tolerates.

rows.stmt: Minimize the percentage of zones assigned an unnecessarily stringent cleanliness classification relative to their actual activity risk
rows.imp_rat: Over-classifying a zone raises cost and operational burden but does not threaten sterility, making it a lower-stakes efficiency concern for this specialist.
rows.sat_rat: Conservative bias in current practice means zones are often over-graded, and few tools challenge that tendency.

rows.stmt: Minimize the degree of over-engineering in a recommended air supply approach beyond what the intended operations require
rows.imp_rat: Over-engineering wastes capital and energy, but as a sterility specialist protection trumps parsimony, so trimming excess is a secondary concern to the core job.
rows.sat_rat: Status-quo practice tends to default to conservative over-design, so today's tools rarely help right-size an air supply approach precisely.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree to which project teams and quality leadership question the authority of my air supply judgments
rows.imp_rat: Being questioned by project teams or quality leadership affects standing but the specialist's job success rests more on technical outcomes than perceived authority.
rows.sat_rat: Established credentials and regulatory backing usually lend the specialist reasonable authority in the status quo.

rows.stmt: Minimize the amount of remaining service life discarded when air handling components are replaced too early
rows.imp_rat: Discarding usable component life is a cost concern the technician notes, but conservative PM schedules exist precisely because contamination risk outranks squeezing full life from a filter or damper.
rows.sat_rat: Status quo relies on fixed time-based PM intervals that intentionally err early, so some life is routinely discarded but the practice is accepted and manageable.

rows.stmt: Reduce the degree of variability in actual service life across identical air handling components
rows.imp_rat: Inconsistent life across identical units complicates planning but is a secondary concern next to keeping air flowing and clean.
rows.sat_rat: Variability is absorbed by conservative scheduling and spares stock rather than actively managed by current tools.
